page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-64 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 256 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core performance ranking in the top 25%. The server demonstrates high memory performance in small-block cached read operations, though uncached large-block read and write bandwidth falls into the average tier. Cryptographic and mathematical operations are highly efficient, supported by hardware-level flags such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

The instance is well-suited for database operations, as evidenced by strong Passmark database scores and high Redis SET throughput of 63.14 million operations per second. It also handles small-to-medium language model inference efficiently. However, a significant performance tradeoff is observed in large-scale language model text generation, where the 70B model text generation speed drops to a poor 2.41 tokens per second, indicating that massive LLM workloads are constrained by the lack of GPU acceleration.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-64 is a general-purpose server configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, running on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 32 physical cores. It features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU execution, high small-block memory bandwidth, and excellent database operation throughput, including 63.14 million Redis SET operations per second. While highly capable for general-purpose computing, software compilation, and small-to-medium model inference, it exhibits a clear performance bottleneck on large-scale LLM text generation, which registers at a poor 2.41 tokens per second. This profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for standard enterprise workloads, database hosting, and parallel processing tasks that do not require local SSD storage or specialized GPU hardware.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-64 is a general-purpose server with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU. It offers strong multi-core performance but is limited in large-scale LLM text generation.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-64 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU general-purpose server delivering strong multi-core processing and high database throughput for enterprise workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores
Equipped with 256 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Delivers strong multi-core CPU and database performance, including high Redis throughput
Lacks local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware
Exhibits performance bottlenecks on large-scale LLM text generation tasks
Best suited for database hosting, multi-threaded applications, and general-purpose enterprise workloads
